ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Creditor'S Claim
Off Calendar Mar-02-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul J. Mori for and with Roman Muros. Robert L. Ferris for Rosalie Gache.
Michael J. Gugliemino for Amando Ibanez and Catalina Cartelino.

The Court informed counsel on what facts were before the Court, what papers were reviewed prior to this hearing and asked counsel to present any evidence to support their claims. Counsel presented oral argument in support of their respective postions.

Having heard oral argument the Court having heard no facts or evidence to stop the estoppel claim. The Court denies the claim of equitable estoppel. The Court to provide a statement of decision.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re Filing Of Petition For Preliminary Distribution And Pending Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-31-2016 Continued To Nov-28-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Ferris for personal representative Rosalie Gache; Paul Mori for beneficiaries Roman and Carmelita Muros; Michael Guglielmino for beneficiaries/objectors Cartenilo and Ibanez.

Mr. Ferris stated that a petition for final distribution, and a substantial creditor's claim has been filed, but the deadline for filing creditor's claims is not until December 27, 2016. He plans to either file an amended petition or a petition for preliminary distribution by February of 2017. Mr. Guglielmino asserted that the creditor's claim was rejected as a matter of law, that his clients are homeless and need action or a distribution. Mr. Mori stated that there is a conflict among the parties regarding any distribution, and stated a desire that a settlement conference be set. The parties agreed to attend pro bono mediation.
The status hearing was continued to November 28, 2016 at 9:00 am in Department 204, with a November 21, 2016 deadline for filing of a status report.

Subsequent to the hearing a Minute Order Referring Case to Pro Bono Mediation was signed. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than January 31, 2017. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by January 24, 2017. A status on mediation is set for February 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. [lgomez]

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Ferris for petitioner Rosalie Gache; Paul Moti for respondents Roman Muros, Jr. and Carmelita Muros; Michael Gugliemino for objectors Catalina Carpenito and Armando Ibanez.

In response to the Court's inquiry regarding the status of the consents by the objectors, Mr. Gugliemino replied that the objectors have withdrawn their consents. Mr. Ferris stated that new assignments of interest had been signed and filed. Mr. Mori asserted that the creditor's claim was filed late in reliance upon the assignments previously filed. Mr. Gugliemino stated that the creditors' claim should be cut in half because of the assignments of interest. Mr. Mori asserted that the Court needs to address the creditors' claim, and asserted that estoppel prevents application of the statute of limitations to that claim. Mr. Ferris stated that the executor needs to either accept or reject the creditors' claim before final distribution can occur. He is hoping to settle the issue. He said that a petition for preliminary distribution may be brought. He added that he doesn't see how a date can be set on the final accounting until action is taken on the creditors' claim. The Court ordered the status hearing off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-21-2016 Continued To Apr-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-07-2016 Continued To Mar-21-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Continued To Mar-07-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Ferris for (administrator) Rosalie Gache. Michael Guglielmino for (objectors) Catalina Carpenito & Armando Ibanez. Paul Mori for Roman Muros, Jr. & Carmelita Muros.

Mr. Guglielmino stated that he has talked to Mr. Ferris, that they have no objection to the distribution with the exception of the Santa Rosa property, that they would agree to preliminary distribution and would like further mediation. Mr. Ferris stated that the issue is of signer/signer, that the petition and order are set to go as of today, that the assignment is valid and that the order was revised to include the third assignment. Mr. Mori stated that they would like a ruling on the assignment and that he has a letter from the other two heirs that they do not object. Mr. Guglielmino stated that his clients were not given the entire document and did not understand the implications, that the property was included in the estate and that the assignment was improper. The Court asked the parties if they had any additional information. Mr. Ferris responded no. Mr. Mori stated that they attached copies of relevant documents to their response and that the standard is fraud and undue influence. Mr. Guglielmino stated that the decedent had provided for his clients well being, that the property they lived in was the property chosen to be sold causing them to become homeless, that there was undue influence when they signed the agreement and that they were only told that signed would expedite the administration of the estate. The Court asked counsel if he were to do a title search would it be beneficial. Mr. Guglielmino stated that if would be beneficial, that they want reimbursement of the administrative costs and that they need information on the loans. The Court asked what would be the expedited time frame to get this mediated. Mr. Guglielmino stated 90 days. Mr. Ferris stated that the nature of the dispute is the assignment which is valid and that he is not sure how a title search would help the argument. Mr. Mori stated that his client paid the mortgage, that he refinanced to obtain better rates, that he is in agreement with Mr. Ferris, that they signed the contract, that his client cannot afford to buy them out and that he is not sure where mediation would get them. The Court asked if it continues the matter to get the title report will it change anything. Mr. Guglielmino stated there probably would be something, that there is enough of a question as to the circumstances regarding the signing and that they would have been unwilling to sign if they had information. The accounting petition was continued to March 7, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for additional documents to be filed and will be under submission at that time.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
